findings.problem: The closing movement cashes both major irreversible turns at once: Mina and Tomas consummate the bargain on-page, and Tomas immediately falsifies the fuel access that will power the next chapter's illegal run. That leaves this chapter feeling over-resolved and steals escalation from the next nocturnal sequence instead of handing it a fresh cliff of pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reallocate the final turn across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:333, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:397, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:569, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:575 so this chapter ends on one irreversible bargain step, not both. Either let snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:397 carry the sexual consummation and defer the actual log lie/fueling beyond this chapter, or keep the operational theft here and cut the physical consummation back so the next chapter still owns a larger seduction/complicity escalation.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised closing unit beginning around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:333 contains exactly one completed irreversible action tied to the bargain and leaves the second as future pressure; fail if the same end-of-chapter sequence still includes full on-page intercourse, completed fuel theft/log falsification, and next-night deployment instructions.

findings.problem: The bargain's leverage beat is staged twice in near-identical terms. Mina is first forced to name what she wants at the counter, then forced to restate the same creek/fuel desire during sex. Because the second scene repeats the same confession pattern instead of discovering a new cost, the long shed sequence partially replays the negotiation rather than escalating it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the pressure functions at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:363, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:377, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:537, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:551. Keep one explicit naming beat, then revise the later passage so it advances through a new consequence already seeded in the chapter: fear of being heard, the ring/Rhea reality, Tomas's class resentment, or the concrete mechanics of the theft. Do not make Mina plainly re-declare the same desire-for-access bargain twice.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the later shed passage no longer requires Mina to restate in plain language that she wants Tomas to take her to the creek or steal the fuel, and instead introduces a new leverage shift or consequence absent from the earlier counter exchange; fail if both cited beats still hinge on Tomas extracting the same verbal confession of what she wants.

findings.problem: The narration leans too often on the same counterfactual hinge: Mina 'should have' felt or done one thing, then does another. Used this many times in one chapter, the construction stops feeling like character texture and starts reading as a reusable sentence template, which flattens the prose and makes her self-rebuke too predictably packaged.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the sentence engines around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:235, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:291, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:473, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:553, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:591 so at least half of these moments land through action, bodily sensation, or a different syntactic shape rather than another 'should have' counterfactual. Preserve Mina's shame and self-correction, but vary the prose logic that carries them.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if no more than two of the cited spots still use a 'should have' counterfactual to announce Mina's expected reaction and at least three of those moments are recast into concrete behavior or sensation; fail if three or more cited passages still rely on the same counterfactual formula.

summary: FAIL. The chapter's dialogue, contraction handling, and unsanitized erotic specificity are strong and remain consistent with the style bible, but the chapter-level sequencing overpays the bargain before the next chapter, the shed scene replays an already-set leverage beat, and the prose shows a noticeable recurring 'should have' sentence template.
